title: Theories about the role of education
summary: 9699 — functionalist, Marxist, and New Right views of education's purpose.
learningObjectives: Explain education's functions: socialisation, selection, skills.
Evaluate New Right and marketisation policies.
Apply Bowles & Gintis and Parsons to case studies.

simpleExplanation.title: School — opportunity or control?
simpleExplanation.summary: Functionalists see schools as meritocratic; Marxists see them reproducing class inequality; the New Right calls for competition and standards.

flashcards.back: Inequality necessary — education selects most talented for key roles.
School mirrors workplace hierarchy — prepares obedient workers.
Marketisation, parental choice, standards — Chubb & Moe.
Education ideological state apparatus reproducing capitalism.

sections.content: **Education theories** (5.1) opens Paper 3. Always **compare perspectives** and judge which fits attainment gap evidence in **6.2–6.4**.

sections.question: Assess Marxist views of the role of education in society. [15 marks]
sections.solution:
**Marxist case:** Bowles & Gintis — **hidden curriculum** (subservience, acceptance of hierarchy); **myth of meritocracy** — credentials legitimise inequality; Althusser — **ISA**; Willis — **lads** resist but end in manual work.

**Criticisms:** **Functionalist** — schools teach **skills** for modern economy; **feminist** — Marxism neglects **patriarchy**; **postmodern** — too deterministic — pupils **not passive**; **social democratic** — education **can** aid mobility (see 5.2).

**Evaluation:** Strong on **structural reproduction** but may **understate agency** and **within-class** variation.

**Conclusion:** Education **reproduces inequality** partly, not wholly.
